text:
## Headline

Nimbus management used its earnings call to reiterate full-year fiscal 2026 revenue guidance, raise the operating-margin outlook, and detail integration plans for the pending Cirrus Analytics acquisition.

## Q&A signals

- Asked about Cirrus integration timing, Daniel Cho said native Cirrus query capabilities would be "generally available inside the Stratus console by the end of fiscal Q4 2026" [Q1].
- On margin drivers, Helena Vargas attributed "about 60 basis points to the hiring slowdown" and the balance to Stratus gross-margin improvement [Q2].
- On hyperscaler competition, Cho said win rates "remained above 75 percent in Q2 in competitive deals we tracked" [Q7].

## Commitments

- Management reiterated full-year fiscal 2026 revenue guidance in the range of "2.55 to 2.60 billion dollars" [K2].
- Non-GAAP operating margin guidance was raised to "approximately 19 percent, up from our prior guidance of 18 percent" [K3], with management also expecting "positive GAAP operating income for the full fiscal year 2026" [K4].
- Nimbus expects to "close the previously announced Cirrus Analytics acquisition by the end of fiscal Q3 2026" [K1], with Stratus Copilot targeted for general availability by end of fiscal Q4 2026 [K7].
